test.name: Write a result sidecar for a positive record search
test.description: Execute plan item pli_001 (1850 census for Patrick Flynn, Schuylkill County). After record_search (with projectPath) stages the matching household and rank_search_matches (with checkAttachments) re-ranks the correct record to the top as a strong match, the skill must retain the raw results: write results/<log_id>.json with the verbatim payload, set the log entry's results_ref and results_available, and write a one-line notes summary.

input.user_message: Search the 1850 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County, per plan item pli_001.

judge_context: The new log entry must carry a non-null results_ref pointing at results/<log_id>.json, and a results/<log_id>.json sidecar must be written containing the verbatim record_search response
The sidecar's returned_count must equal the number of results in its payload
results_available should reflect the upstream total-match count, and notes should be a one-line human summary of what the search returned

Triage flow: after record_search stages results (called with projectPath), the skill should call rank_search_matches with the staged.resultsRef, the subject id (I1), and checkAttachments: true — NOT per-result same_person or a separate source_attachments call (rank_search_matches folds both in). The fixture re-ranks the correct record (Patrick Flynn, ark MXHY-TP4) to matchRank 1 with a strong match score, confirming the positive match whose raw results the sidecar then retains.

IMPORTANT — How file writes work in this skill: The skill uses the baseline Write/Edit tool to write research.json and results/ files. These are NOT MCP tool calls and will NOT appear in the MCP tool calls section. Research.json modifications appear in the file-change diff. results/ sidecars are NEWLY CREATED files — they do NOT appear in the file-change diff (only modifications to existing files are diffed).

Log quality: Score 3 if the file-change diff shows research.json was modified with a new log entry added. Do NOT require the model to print the raw JSON log entry in its text response. Do NOT require a Write MCP tool call — the Write/Edit tool is a baseline tool, not an MCP tool.

Sidecar correctness: Score 3 if the skill's text response references writing results/<log_id>.json (e.g., 'writing results/log_005.json', 'Sidecar: results/log_005.json', 'retained at results/log_005.json'). Do NOT reduce this score because results/ does not appear in the file-change diff (it's a new file, not a diff). Do NOT require a Write MCP tool call — sidecar writes use the baseline Write tool.

Tool Arguments / Result triage: Score 3 if record_search (with projectPath) and rank_search_matches (with stagedResultsRef, subjectId, checkAttachments) were both called with appropriate parameters. A validate_research_schema call with a project path after writing is acceptable and expected — do not penalize it. Per-result same_person and a separate source_attachments call are NOT expected (rank_search_matches folds both in). Score Result triage 3 if the skill identifies the top-ranked record as a strong match and notes the key supporting attributes — tool calls beyond record_search and rank_search_matches are not required.

Completeness: Score 3 if the search was executed, the staged results were re-ranked via rank_search_matches, the sidecar was written, and the log entry was appended. All required outputs (sidecar + log entry) are complete.
